description: Trigger evals for the claude-config-audit skill. Half should-trigger (the user genuinely wants the audit workflow), half should-not-trigger (near-misses that share keywords but actually need a different tool). Realistic phrasings — the kinds of things a user actually types.

evals.prompt: im looking at my plugin list in claude code and i can barely scroll through it anymore, i swear half of these i installed once and never used. can you help me clean this up?
evals.expected_output: skill triggers; runs prerequisite check + discovery; offers both audit halves

evals.prompt: audit my ~/.claude setup
evals.expected_output: skill triggers

evals.prompt: Every time I start Claude Code my system prompt is huge with all these skill descriptions for stuff I don't even use. Is there a way to figure out which ones I actually invoke vs. which are just noise? My ~/.claude/skills/ has like 40 things in it.
evals.expected_output: skill triggers; emphasises evidence from session history not just listing

evals.prompt: spring clean my claude install, it's a mess
evals.expected_output: skill triggers

evals.prompt: I have rules in ~/.claude/rules/ that I wrote 6 months ago and I can't remember which ones still apply. Can you go through them and tell me which to keep, which need updating, and whether there are gaps?
evals.expected_output: skill triggers; runs the rules half (skips skills half if user explicitly only wanted rules)

evals.prompt: /audit-skills
evals.expected_output: slash command triggers the skills half directly

evals.prompt: doctor my claude config, something feels bloated
evals.expected_output: skill triggers; doctor/bloated/config combo is a strong signal

evals.prompt: I keep getting permission prompts for the same Bash commands over and over. Also my hooks list is getting unwieldy — can we go through ~/.claude/settings.json and figure out what's actually pulling weight?
evals.expected_output: skill triggers; security-pass agent particularly relevant for hooks/settings audit

evals.prompt: before i clear context can you take a quick look at my installed plugins and tell me which ones to nuke?
evals.expected_output: skill triggers even with terse phrasing — 'installed plugins' + 'nuke' is unambiguous

evals.prompt: i ran this audit thing 6 weeks ago and i want to do another pass — can it just show me what's changed since last time rather than re-asking everything?
evals.expected_output: skill triggers; uses audit-history.py diff to surface only deltas

evals.prompt: audit my AWS bill, it's gone up 30% this month and I want to know what's costing me
evals.expected_output: should NOT trigger — 'audit' keyword but completely different domain (cloud cost, not Claude Code config)

evals.prompt: review my plugins for the wordpress site I'm building, are these the right ones for an e-commerce setup?
evals.expected_output: should NOT trigger — 'plugins' keyword but wordpress, not Claude Code

evals.prompt: I want to install the github plugin in claude code, can you walk me through it?
evals.expected_output: should NOT trigger — install workflow, not audit. /plugin install is the right tool here.

evals.prompt: clean up the rules in my .eslintrc, half of them are duplicating the prettier config and we keep getting conflicts
evals.expected_output: should NOT trigger — 'clean up rules' keyword but ESLint, not Claude Code rules

evals.prompt: delete the user account for jane@example.com from our database, she requested account closure last week
evals.expected_output: should NOT trigger — 'delete' keyword but a database operation against user data, completely unrelated

evals.prompt: spring clean my home directory, there are old downloads and screenshots from 2024 i never look at
evals.expected_output: should NOT trigger — 'spring clean' keyword but home dir cleanup, not Claude Code config. fd or find is the tool.

evals.prompt: what skills are needed for a senior frontend engineer role? i'm updating my CV
evals.expected_output: should NOT trigger — 'skills' keyword but career advice, not Claude Code skills

evals.prompt: review the security of my claude code settings — are there any tokens leaking or hooks doing something dodgy?
evals.expected_output: EDGE CASE — could trigger because the security-pass agent does exactly this. Acceptable to trigger or to point at the security-pass agent specifically. Not a clean negative.

evals.prompt: claude isn't loading my CLAUDE.md, can you help debug why
evals.expected_output: should NOT trigger — debugging a load failure isn't an audit. Suggest /InstructionsLoaded hook or settings.json check instead.

evals.prompt: trim the leading whitespace from this code block, it's confusing the linter
evals.expected_output: should NOT trigger — 'trim' keyword but code formatting, completely unrelated
